name: Joint Base Andrews
tagline: Home of Air Force One and presidential airlift, just outside Washington, D.C.
_note: Joint Base Andrews-NAF Washington (Air Force lead + Navy), Prince George's County MD, DC metro. CONUS full template (BAH MHA DC053 Washington DC Metro; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). Home of the 89th Airlift Wing / presidential airlift (Air Force One). Joint base -> picked up by isJoint() via name. Medical: Malcolm Grow Medical Clinics & Surgery Center. Facts verified from official sources (jba.af.mil, Malcolm Grow/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Maryland is TRICARE East. High-cost DC metro market. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 20746 (Suitland/Camp Springs).

data.Branch: U.S. Air Force (lead) with U.S. Navy (NAF Washington)
data.Location: Camp Springs, MD (Prince George's County, DC metro)
data.Also hosts: Naval Air Facility Washington and many tenant units
data.Setting: Washington, D.C. suburbs (high cost of living)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Joint Base Andrews is best known as the home of Air Force One and presidential airlift, flown by the 89th Airlift Wing. It's a joint installation, Air Force-led and combined with Naval Air Facility Washington, hosting a wide range of tenant units.
Just outside Washington, D.C., in Prince George's County, it offers proximity to the national capital region, with everything that brings: career opportunities, traffic, and a high cost of living.

sections.title: Living in the D.C. Metro: Commute Is Everything

data.subtitle: Andrews sits in the expensive, traffic-heavy D.C. metro. The defining choice is balancing commute (and the Beltway) against price across the Maryland suburbs.
intro: The national capital region is costly and congested, so where you live is mostly about commute. On-base housing is in demand; off base, families spread across the Maryland suburbs south and east of the city.
Closer-in areas cost more; heading farther into southern Maryland trades a longer drive for more space and lower prices.

recommendations.advice: Camp Springs, Clinton, and Upper Marlboro sit close to the base in Prince George's County.
Waldorf and southern Charles County offer newer homes and more room, with a longer drive.
Bowie and the Crofton/Anne Arundel area are popular with families; weigh the commute and traffic.
On-base housing is convenient in this pricey market and in demand; get on the list early.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: As a high-profile installation, Andrews has strict access control. Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, especially around official movements, so confirm the current details on the official Joint Base Andrews site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Joint Base Andrews has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods, a convenient option in the high-cost D.C. metro.
Demand is high, so eligibility and waitlists matter. Start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and timelines.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA DC053 (Washington, DC Metro)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Prince George's County Public Schools serves the area around the base, with neighboring districts (Charles, Anne Arundel) depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Clinic: Malcolm Grow Medical Clinics & Surgery Center
data.Care: Outpatient primary, specialty, and surgical care on base; inpatient care via Walter Reed (Bethesda) and the DC-area network

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and in the expensive D.C. metro the math is tougher than at most duty stations.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
Home prices closer in are out of comfortable reach for your budget.
You want flexibility in a costly, fast-moving market.
On-base housing or renting keeps you closer in without overextending.
buy: You expect a long tour or repeat assignments in the capital region, which is common.
You can afford the entry price and want to build equity with your VA loan.
You've found a Maryland suburb and commute that work and plan to hold the home.
You're comfortable being a long-distance landlord if you PCS before selling.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, and the D.C. metro's high prices make buying a bigger commitment. Many families rent or take on-base housing, especially on shorter tours, while those expecting repeat DC assignments often buy. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your commute, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. In a big, expensive metro, starting early pays off.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: On-base housing is in demand; get on the list early and get the office's read on the metro before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Maryland vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Prince George's, Charles, or Anne Arundel County) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Malcolm Grow or network providers.

data.q: Is this where Air Force One is based?
data.a: Yes. Joint Base Andrews is home to the 89th Airlift Wing, which flies presidential and senior-leader airlift, including Air Force One.
Across the Maryland suburbs. Camp Springs, Clinton, and Upper Marlboro are close; Waldorf and southern Maryland offer more space; Bowie/Crofton are popular for schools. On-base housing is in demand.
Yes. It's the D.C. metro, one of the costlier and most congested regions, so commute and budget drive where you live.
Andrews is MHA DC053 (Washington, DC Metro). Use the calculator above for your grade.
